title: Redesigning Onboarding for 2M Users
summary: Rebuilt first-run experience for a consumer app with 2M MAU, cutting time-to-value and lifting activation by a third.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.body: New users dropped off within the first 90 seconds. Analytics showed 61% never completed setup, and support tickets clustered around unclear next steps. Leadership set a goal: improve week-1 activation without increasing acquisition spend.

sections.heading: Discovery
sections.body: I ran 14 user interviews, mapped the existing funnel, and partnered with design on a jobs-to-be-done workshop. We found users wanted a fast win—not a tour of every feature. The old flow asked for permissions and profile data before demonstrating value.

sections.caption: Before/after onboarding flow — value-first path highlighted in orange

sections.heading: My Approach
sections.body: We reframed onboarding around a single 'aha' moment: completing one core action in under 60 seconds. I wrote a one-pager with success metrics, ran a phased rollout (5% → 25% → 100%), and built a dashboard tracking activation, retention, and support volume by cohort.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Activation rose 34%, time-to-value dropped 18 seconds, and support tickets for 'getting started' fell 27%. The framework became the template for two additional product lines.
